stories.headline: Hiker discovers human remains in Douglas County, investigation underway
stories.summary:
A hiker found human remains in Douglas County on Wednesday, prompting a response from the Douglas County Sheriff's Office and the Douglas County Search and Rescue Team. Investigators searched the surrounding area for additional evidence, though authorities have not yet released details about the identity of the remains or the circumstances of the discovery.

The Douglas County Sheriff's Office has not indicated whether foul play is suspected. The investigation remains active and ongoing.

stories.headline: Auto shop operator launches fundraiser after Thornton fire destroys roughly 100 vehicles
stories.summary:
Jason Rol, operator of Rol Race Motorsports on the 480 block of West 84th Avenue in Thornton, has launched a GoFundMe campaign after Wednesday's fast-moving grass fire destroyed approximately 100 vehicles stored at his automotive restoration shop. Rol said many of the cars consumed in the blaze belonged to customers who had entrusted them to his care, calling the aftermath heartbreaking for himself, his customers and neighbors.

Within 21 hours of the fundraiser's creation, the campaign had already raised $3,400 toward its $6,000 goal. The fire, which spread rapidly through a grassy area behind the shop amid dry and windy conditions, also injured four firefighters and one civilian, though none critically. Authorities continue to investigate the cause of the blaze.

stories.headline: Denver area events for Friday, Feb. 27
stories.summary:
Denver and surrounding communities have a range of cultural and entertainment events scheduled for Friday, Feb. 27. Highlights include comedian Michelle Buteau at the Paramount Theatre, an Opera Colorado Grand Gala at the Denver Performing Arts Complex, and a performance by the Lone Tree Symphony Orchestra at Lone Tree Arts Center.

Additional events include the drama DJ Drama with Amtrac at Mission Ballroom, Cleo Parker Robinson Dance at the Parsons Theatre in Northglenn, and several live music shows at venues across the metro including the Gothic Theatre, Fox Theatre in Boulder and the Oriental Theater. The Denver Center's production of "The Music Man" continues its run at the Buell Theatre.

stories.headline: Nuggets linked to Khris Middleton on buyout market ahead of Sunday's playoff-eligibility deadline
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets have shown interest in former NBA champion Khris Middleton as a potential buyout-market addition, according to NBA insider Marc Stein and Denver Post reporter Bennett Durando. Middleton, currently with the Dallas Mavericks, is weighing whether to seek a buyout before Sunday's March 1 deadline, after which a player waived by a new team would not be playoff-eligible.

Durando reported that the Nuggets have been pursuing forward depth on the buyout market and that Middleton's size on the wing and ball-handling ability match the profile Denver has targeted. Middleton, a three-time All-Star, holds career averages of 16.3 points, 4.7 rebounds and 3.9 assists per game, and won an NBA Championship with the Milwaukee Bucks in 2021. Kyle Anderson, another name linked to the Nuggets' search, signed with the Minnesota Timberwolves on Friday.

stories.headline: Jamal Murray listed as questionable for Nuggets' game at Oklahoma City Thunder
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets listed All-Star guard Jamal Murray as questionable Friday for their road game against the Oklahoma City Thunder after Murray left Wednesday's 103-84 victory over the Boston Celtics early and did not return. Peyton Watson and Aaron Gordon were ruled out for the contest.

Murray is averaging 25.1 points, 7.4 assists and 4.3 rebounds per game this season and is a key figure in Denver's playoff push. The Nuggets enter the game as the Western Conference's fourth seed with a 37-22 record. The Thunder, the top seed in the West at 45-15, will have Shai Gilgeous-Alexander available after he was cleared to return from a nine-game absence due to an abdominal strain.

stories.headline: Colorado Avalanche linked to Vancouver Canucks defenseman Tyler Myers ahead of trade deadline
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche have been linked to Vancouver Canucks defenseman Tyler Myers ahead of the NHL trade deadline, according to Rick Dhaliwal of CHEKTV in Vancouver. The Avs, who traded defenseman Sam Girard and a draft pick to Pittsburgh earlier this week in exchange for Brett Kulak, have approximately $9 million in cap space available.

Myers, who stands 6-foot-8 and weighs 229 pounds, serves as an alternate captain in Vancouver and contributes primarily as a physical, defensive presence, recording 65 hits and 91 blocked shots this season. He is also considered a strong penalty killer, an area where Colorado already ranks among the league's best. Other names linked to the Avalanche include Ryan O'Reilly and former Av Nazem Kadri.

stories.headline: Mountain highways shut down by snow and ice Thursday morning, I-70 affected
stories.summary:
Snow and ice forced the closure of several Colorado mountain highways Thursday morning, including a stretch of westbound Interstate 70 between Empire Junction and Georgetown, according to the Colorado Department of Transportation. The I-70 closure was lifted shortly before 9:30 a.m., but heavy traffic persisted in both directions between Silverthorne and Golden throughout the morning.

The closures came amid a broader winter weather pattern affecting the state's mountain corridors. CDOT cameras showed wet and snowy road conditions across mountain passes, creating hazardous travel for commuters and visitors heading to and from ski areas.

stories.headline: Sierra Space names Dan Jablonsky as CEO after yearlong search
stories.summary:
Louisville-based space company Sierra Space has appointed Dan Jablonsky as its new chief executive officer following a search that lasted approximately one year, the Denver Business Journal reported. Jablonsky previously served as CEO of Maxar Technologies, the Westminster-based satellite and geospatial intelligence company, and most recently held a leadership role at Ursa Major.

Sierra Space, a subsidiary of Sierra Nevada Corporation, is developing the Dream Chaser spaceplane and is among Colorado's most prominent aerospace companies. The appointment of a locally rooted executive with deep experience in the satellite and space sectors signals continued investment in the company's growth trajectory.

stories.headline: Red flag warning issued again for Denver metro and Front Range on Friday amid continued fire weather
stories.summary:
The National Weather Service in Boulder issued a Red Flag Warning for the Denver metro area and much of the Front Range on Friday, running from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m., as dry and windy conditions persisted for a second consecutive day. Forecasters cited low relative humidity and strong winds as key drivers of elevated fire danger across the urban corridor and northeastern plains.

The warning follows a week of unseasonably warm and dry weather across the region, with temperatures running roughly 20 degrees above normal. The conditions contributed to two significant grass fires in the Denver metro area within the past two days, including Wednesday's blaze in Thornton that injured five people and damaged businesses. Denver Water officials have also flagged declining reservoir levels as the warm, dry winter continues.

stories.headline: Suzanne Morphew's daughters seek return of her remains as murder trial approaches
stories.summary:
The adult daughters of Suzanne Morphew, who disappeared six years ago in Chaffee County, are seeking the return of their mother's remains from investigators, the Colorado Springs Gazette reported. Barry Morphew, Suzanne's husband, has been charged with first-degree murder in connection with her death. Mallory and Macy Morphew have filed a request with investigators who currently have custody of the remains.

Suzanne Morphew went missing in May 2020 and her remains were later discovered. The case has drawn widespread attention in Colorado due to its prolonged investigative timeline and the charges against Barry Morphew, who was previously arrested, had charges dropped, and was later recharged.

stories.headline: Rockies win first spring training game as Brenton Doyle leads offense against Diamondbacks
stories.summary:
The Colorado Rockies picked up their first win of spring training, defeating the Arizona Diamondbacks behind a strong performance from outfielder Brenton Doyle. The victory gives the Rockies their first positive result as they ramp up preparations for the 2026 MLB season.

Doyle, who has emerged as one of the organization's key young position players in recent seasons, led the offensive effort in the Cactus League contest. The Rockies enter the spring facing questions about roster construction and direction as the franchise continues efforts to build toward competitiveness.

stories.headline: Broncos' Davis Webb confirmed as new offensive play-caller at NFL Combine
stories.summary:
Denver Broncos head coach Sean Payton confirmed at the NFL Combine that offensive coordinator Davis Webb will take over play-calling duties for the 2026 season, ending speculation about the role. The announcement represents a significant shift in the team's offensive structure as Denver heads into an offseason with high expectations following an 11-game winning streak to close last season.

General manager George Paton also spoke at the Combine and is entering the final year of his current contract with the organization. Payton said he was unconcerned about reported worries surrounding the NFL's tush push rule, dismissing the issue as not a significant factor in Denver's offensive planning.
